title: cleomella hillmanii
intro: Cleomella hillmanii, also known as Hill's Clematis, is a flowering plant native to New Zealand and Australia, characterized by its delicate white or pink flowers and twining habit. The plant is said to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Cleomella hillmanii include soothing skin irritations, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory problems, such as colds and bronchitis. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and poultices, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
